title: I'm a prompt engineer. Here's what I actually do all day
post:
People ask me what I actually do as a prompt engineer. Let me pull back the curtain.

Most of my time is spent:

- Translating vague business needs into precise AI instructions
- Testing prompts hundreds of times to find failure modes
- Building prompt templates that non-technical users can apply
- Analyzing when AI fails and diagnosing why
- Staying current on model capabilities and limitations

It's equal parts linguist, psychologist, and quality assurance engineer.

The biggest misconception? That I write clever creative prompts. The reality is more mundane—I write clear, structured instructions with explicit constraints.

The best prompts aren't clever. They're comprehensive.

Curious about what a career in prompt engineering actually looks like?
